title: Stop Social Media Account Takeovers

modules.title: How Hackers Target Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook Accounts
modules.sub: Mapping the real attack methods used against everyday users
modules.description: Learners explore the most common tactics attackers use to compromise social media accounts, including credential stuffing, phishing, and account cloning. By the end of the module, learners can identify which attack types they are most exposed to based on their current habits and account activity.

modules.title: AI-Powered Phishing: Fake Support Pages, Deepfakes, and SIM Swaps
modules.sub: Recognizing AI-generated scams designed to steal your access
modules.description: Learners examine how artificial intelligence is being used to create convincing fake customer support interactions, deepfake video verification traps, and SIM swap schemes. They practice spotting red flags in realistic example scenarios so they can confidently distinguish legitimate platform communications from sophisticated fakes.

modules.title: Your Digital Footprint: What You're Giving Attackers for Free
modules.sub: Auditing the personal data you're unknowingly exposing online
modules.description: Learners conduct a guided audit of their own publicly visible information across social platforms, assessing what details could be harvested and weaponized by an attacker. They make deliberate decisions about which profile fields, posts, and connections to restrict or remove to reduce their exposure.

modules.title: Locking Down Your Accounts: Passwords, MFA, and Backup Codes
modules.sub: Applying the security settings that actually stop account takeovers
modules.description: Learners walk through a step-by-step process for strengthening their Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook accounts using strong unique passwords, multi-factor authentication, and saved backup codes. They leave the module with concrete changes made — or a clear checklist to complete — rather than just theoretical knowledge.

modules.title: What to Do When You're Already Locked Out
modules.sub: Executing a calm, effective recovery plan under pressure
modules.description: Learners work through a prioritized recovery playbook for regaining access to a compromised or locked account, including how to navigate each platform's official appeal and identity verification processes. They also identify which steps to take immediately to limit damage, such as notifying contacts and revoking connected app permissions.

modules.title: Protecting Loved Ones: Helping Non-Tech-Savvy Family Members
modules.sub: Translating security basics into guidance anyone can follow
modules.description: Learners consider the specific vulnerabilities faced by older relatives and less tech-experienced contacts who are frequent targets of social media scams. They adapt the course's core security practices into simple, patient conversations and easy-to-follow setup walkthroughs they can share with family members.

modules.title: Building Habits That Last: A Simple Personal Security Routine
modules.sub: Turning one-time fixes into an ongoing personal security practice
modules.description: Learners design a lightweight, repeatable personal security routine — including monthly check-ins, alert settings, and password hygiene habits — that fits realistically into their daily life. They commit to a specific schedule and set of actions so that account protection becomes automatic rather than reactive.

synopsis: Thousands of people lose access to their Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook accounts every day — and AI-generated fake customer support, deepfake verification scams, and automated credential stuffing make it easier than ever for attackers to strike. This course gives everyday users a clear, jargon-free path to securing their accounts and recovering quickly if the worst happens.
rationale: Social media account takeover is one of the highest-volume AI-assisted attacks targeting ordinary consumers, yet accessible, action-oriented guidance remains almost entirely absent from mainstream digital literacy resources.
learning_outcome: Learners can immediately audit and strengthen their social media account security settings, recognize the AI-generated scams most likely to target them, and execute a recovery plan if their account is compromised.
source_signals: How Hackers Hack Instagram | Full Ethical Hacking Course in Hindi | Cybersecurity Awareness
